policy.title: RBI Issues Final Guidelines on Climate Risk Management for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI released binding climate risk management guidelines requiring all scheduled commercial banks to integrate climate scenarios into ICAAP by March 2027, with mandatory PD adjustments for high-exposure sectors including agriculture, real estate, and infrastructure. Banks must submit first climate stress test results by Q4 2026.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Top 500 Listed Companies with Assurance Mandate
policy.description: SEBI mandated BRSR Core reporting for top 500 companies by market cap starting FY 2026-27, with reasonable assurance required on 9 key ESG metrics including Scope 1, 2 emissions and water intensity. Third-party assurance providers must be empaneled with SEBI by August 2026.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI Releases Exposure Draft on Index-Based Insurance Products for Climate Perils
policy.description: IRDAI published draft guidelines permitting insurers to design parametric products triggered by satellite-verified indices for rainfall deficit, cyclone wind speed, and flood inundation depth. Consultation closes May 15, 2026, with final rules expected by July.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee Finalizes Pillar 3 Climate Disclosure Framework
policy.description: BCBS issued final Pillar 3 disclosure requirements for climate-related financial risks, mandating quantitative metrics on physical and transition risk exposures by asset class. Implementation begins January 2027 for G-SIBs, with phased rollout to other banks through 2028.

policy.title: MCA Confirms Phased ISSB Adoption Roadmap Aligned with BRSR
policy.description: Ministry of Corporate Affairs announced India will adopt IFRS S1 and S2 sustainability standards through a modified BRSR framework, targeting interoperability by FY 2028-29. A technical working group will issue mapping guidance between BRSR Core and ISSB metrics by December 2026.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Pathways
policy.description: NGFS published updated climate scenarios incorporating latest IPCC AR7 findings, with granular physical risk pathways for South Asia showing 2.1°C warming by 2050 under Current Policies. New variables include compound extreme events and agricultural productivity shocks.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for Physical Risk Assessment Pilot
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a strategic partnership with HDFC Bank to pilot physical climate risk scoring across the bank's commercial real estate portfolio in coastal Maharashtra and Gujarat. The pilot will integrate Jupiter's ClimateScore data into HDFC's credit underwriting workflow.

competitors.title: Sprih Raises $18M Series B to Expand Bank ESG Solutions
competitors.description: Bengaluru-based Sprih closed an $18M Series B led by Accel and Prosus Ventures to expand its ESG data platform for Indian banks and NBFCs. The company plans to launch a climate risk module targeting RBI compliance by Q3 2026.

competitors.title: Moody's Launches Physical Risk Scores for Indian Banking Portfolios
competitors.description: Moody's ESG Solutions released India-specific physical risk scores covering flood, cyclone, and heat stress at PIN code level, designed for bank portfolio screening. The product integrates with Moody's credit analytics suite and targets PSU banks preparing for RBI climate stress tests.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory Partners with Yes Bank for Natural Capital Risk Assessment
competitors.description: GIST Advisory signed an MOU with Yes Bank to integrate natural capital and biodiversity risk metrics into the bank's agricultural lending framework. The partnership will cover crop loan portfolios across five water-stressed states.

competitors.title: XDI Releases Asset-Level Physical Risk API for Emerging Market Banks
competitors.description: XDI launched an API product providing asset-level physical risk ratings for bank portfolios in India, Southeast Asia, and Africa, covering 12 climate perils with 100-meter resolution. Pricing targets mid-market banks and NBFCs with portfolio sizes under $10B.

competitors.title: Watershed Raises $200M Series D, Announces Financial Services Climate Module
competitors.description: Watershed closed a $200M Series D at a $3.5B valuation and announced a dedicated module for financial institutions covering financed emissions calculation and PCAF-aligned portfolio analytics. The company signaled expansion into India and Brazil in 2026.

research.title: Climate Risk and Bank Capital: Transmission Channels and Supervisory Responses
research.description: BIS Working Paper analyzing how physical climate risks transmit to bank capital through PD and LGD channels, finding that unhedged flood exposure can increase portfolio losses by 15-40 basis points under severe scenarios. Recommends supervisors mandate granular hazard-exposure mapping.

research.title: Scaling Parametric Insurance in South Asia: Lessons from Flood and Drought Products
research.description: Swiss Re report examining parametric insurance adoption in India, Bangladesh, and Pakistan, finding basis risk concerns and payout speed as key adoption drivers. Recommends hybrid index designs combining satellite and ground-station triggers to reduce basis risk below 15%.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Sustainable Finance
research.description: IFC study quantifying ESG data coverage gaps across 45 emerging markets, finding India has disclosure data for only 38% of listed companies and 12% of private firms. Recommends development of alternative data proxies and sector-specific estimation models.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk Assessment for Indian Banking Sector
research.description: CRISIL analysis mapping physical risk exposure across Indian bank loan books, estimating 18% of PSU bank agricultural portfolios and 12% of private bank real estate portfolios face high or very high physical risk. Coastal Gujarat, Odisha, and Andhra Pradesh identified as priority exposure zones.

research.title: Climate Scenario Analysis: Practice and Challenges for Central Banks
research.description: NGFS technical paper reviewing climate scenario analysis implementations across 28 central banks, noting most struggle to translate macro scenarios to asset-level PD impacts. Calls for development of sector-specific damage functions calibrated to regional hazard data.

research.title: Insuring Climate Risks in Agriculture: The Role of Index Insurance
research.description: World Bank report analyzing index insurance schemes across 15 countries, finding that products with payout triggers calibrated to local agronomic conditions achieve 40% higher renewal rates. India's PMFBY hybrid model cited as scalable template for other emerging markets.

ourRead.headline: RBI's binding climate stress test deadline creates a 9-month window for StepChange to become the default PD/LGD infrastructure for Indian banks.
body: This week's signals converge on a single theme: Indian regulators are moving from guidance to mandates, and the compliance clock is now ticking. RBI's final climate risk guidelines (policy-1), SEBI's expanded BRSR assurance requirements (policy-2), and IRDAI's parametric insurance framework (policy-3) collectively signal that climate risk is graduating from voluntary ESG to hard prudential regulation. Banks, insurers, and corporates face overlapping deadlines between Q4 2026 and March 2027.

The RBI guidelines are the most consequential development for StepChange. The mandate for climate-adjusted PD modelling in ICAAP, with stress test submissions due Q4 2026, creates urgent demand for exactly what we sell: hazard-to-credit translation. The CRISIL report (research-4) quantifies the exposure—18% of PSU agricultural portfolios at high risk—but banks lack the granular models to operationalize this into capital planning. StepChange should position its PD/LGD tools as the bridge between NGFS scenarios (policy-6) and RBI-compliant stress test outputs, emphasizing our ability to calibrate damage functions to Indian regional hazard data where global competitors fall short.

The competitor signals demand immediate attention. Jupiter's HDFC partnership (comp-1) is a direct incursion into our core market, and Moody's India-specific physical risk scores (comp-3) lower the barrier for banks to choose incumbent vendors. However, both are adapting global models to India rather than building India-first. Sprih's Series B (comp-2) signals growing investor appetite for India ESG infrastructure, validating our market, but their climate module is 6+ months away. XDI's emerging market API (comp-5) and Watershed's financial services push (comp-6) confirm that global players see the opportunity but lack local regulatory context. StepChange's edge is regulatory fluency: we can deliver RBI-aligned outputs, not just hazard data.

The research signals reinforce our product roadmap. The BIS working paper (research-1) validates our PD/LGD transmission approach, while the IFC data gaps report (research-3) underscores our Global South data bet—if India has only 12% private firm ESG coverage, alternative data and estimation models become essential. The Swiss Re parametric report (research-2) provides a playbook for our insurance product: hybrid triggers and basis risk below 15% should be our design benchmark. We should reference this research in our IRDAI consultation response before May 15.

Watch: IRDAI's May consultation deadline is the near-term milestone—a strong submission positions StepChange as a technical partner for parametric product design. Action: Prioritize a product demo package for 3-5 PSU banks facing the Q4 2026 stress test deadline; they have the most exposure and the least internal capacity. Jupiter's HDFC pilot means private banks may already be spoken for.
